title: Cephaelis violacea
taxonomy: Cephaelis violacea is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family includes over 600 genera and approximately 13,000 species of flowering plants. Cephaelis violacea is further classified within the genus Cephaelis, which consists of about 50 species of flowering plants. The species Cephaelis violacea is native to Central and South America and is a member of the order Gentianales. The classification of Cephaelis violacea provides essential information for its identification and conservation.
uses: Cephaelis violacea, also known as the Yacruna tree, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The Yacruna tree's bark and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. In addition, the plant's extracts have been used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and stomach pain. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Cephaelis violacea and its potential applications in modern medicine.

intro: Cephaelis violacea, also known as the 'Wood Rose', is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis violacea include alkaloids, specifically isopentenyladenine and caffeine-related compounds, as well as other xanthine derivatives, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cephaelis violacea is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and inflammation.
